assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Sofia, you're structuring contractor controls post-round — and the next investor will look at this more closely. Mellow CoR: we take on documents, KYC, payment chain with audit trail. We don't try to close your legal risk entirely — we remove the grey zone in the payment chain. What's the weakest point right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Sofia, you're structuring contractor controls post-round — and the next investor will look at this more closely. Mellow CoR: we take on documents, KYC, payment chain with audit trail. We don't try to close your legal risk entirely — we remove the grey zone in the payment chain. objections.objection: Ok, you noticed the round context. Right away then: what exactly does Mellow make more structured before diligence, and where does your scope end?
Ok. But if a payment gets stuck again, what's the operational process? Is there an SLA or just more promises?
Separately: contractor docs, tax handling and how that's explained to the next investor on diligence?
Fine. Send the flow and audit story — brief, no fluff. If it's real, we can talk for 20 minutes.

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: I'll send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and how the audit trail looks before diligence. If it speaks to your situation — let's do a short call.
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Sofia, you're structuring contractor controls post-round — and the next investor will look at this more closely. Mellow CoR: we take on documents, KYC, payment chain with audit trail. We don't try to close your legal risk entirely — we remove the grey zone in the payment chain. What's the weakest point right now?
Ok, you noticed the round context. Right away then: what exactly does Mellow make more structured before diligence, and where does your scope end?

Mellow's scope is specific: KYC, contractor documentation, payment chain, and audit trail on each transaction — that's on our side. What we don't take on: misclassification risk and your strategic legal decisions. That's the boundary, in the contract, in writing.
Ok. But if a payment gets stuck again, what's the operational process? Is there an SLA or just more promises?

On SLA: a named escalation owner, defined response time in the contract, incident trail documented. Not general support — a process with accountability. What else do you need to understand before moving forward?
Separately: contractor docs, tax handling and how that's explained to the next investor on diligence?
I'll send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and how the audit trail looks before diligence. If it speaks to your situation — let's do a short call.
Fine. Send the flow and audit story — brief, no fluff. If it's real, we can talk for 20 minutes.
I'll send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and how the audit trail looks before diligence. If it speaks to your situation — let's do a short call.
Ok. Send it over — I'll review.
